ByteDance's Doubao large model team announced the open-source release of UI-TARS- 1.5, a multi-modal agent built upon a vision-language model capable of efficiently executing various tasks in a virtual world. This model achieved state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance across seven typical GUI benchmark evaluations, showcasing for the first time its long-term reasoning capabilities in games and interactive abilities in open environments. This open-source project marks significant progress in multi-modal agent technology for GUI operation and game reasoning. UI-TARS-1.5 builds upon ByteDance's previously proposed native agent solution, UI-TARS, further enhancing the model's high-level reasoning capabilities through reinforcement learning, enabling the model to "think" before "acting." This "think-then-act" mechanism significantly improves the model's generalization ability when facing unfamiliar environments and tasks, resulting in better performance than previous SOTA models across multiple mainstream evaluation benchmarks. For example, in GUI Grounding ability assessment, UI-TARS-1.5 achieved an accuracy of 61.6% on the challenging ScreenSpotPro benchmark, surpassing Claude's 27.7%, CUA's 23.4%, and the previous SOTA model's 43.6%. å¾®ä¿¡æˆªå›¾_20250418080056.png UI-TARS-1.5 also demonstrated excellent performance in game tasks. The team selected 14 diverse mini-games from poki.com for testing, allowing a maximum of 1000 interaction steps per game. UI-TARS-1.5 not only exhibited good performance in these games but also showed stable scalability in inference time.
